title: DrugOOD ChEMBL-Derived Drug Discovery OOD Benchmark

data_urls.access_notes: Project page links realized exemplar datasets and curator details.
Use official loaders and configuration files where possible.

instructions: Use the DrugOOD repository and project page to select a realized exemplar dataset.
Record task, noise level, domain annotation, ChEMBL version, and split definition.
Start with one curated subset rather than reconstructing all ChEMBL-derived data from scratch.

scale.download_size: variable by realized dataset; use one selected subset for first audits

recommended_axes.name: assay_environment_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Similarity of assay or measurement environments using assay IDs, target IDs, or metadata embeddings.

recommended_axes.name: chemical_scaffold_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Molecular fingerprint or scaffold similarity between test molecules and training molecules.

recommended_axes.name: target_family_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Protein target family or sequence similarity when target annotations are available.

useful_for: Targeted operating-point and full-curve audits for drug-discovery domain generalization.
Testing whether assay-domain shifts or chemical novelty drive molecular model failure.
Comparing SPECTRA curves against established DrugOOD environment splits.
leakage_risks: Duplicate or near-duplicate molecules can cross environment splits.
Assay labels can encode target or measurement artifacts.
Reconstructed ChEMBL subsets must pin source version and preprocessing.
